name: ipsecPolicyDatabaseAdd
title: ipsecPolicyDatabaseAdd
syntax: long ipsecPolicyDatabaseAdd(IP_Endpoint source, dword sourcePrefix, IP_Endoint destination, dword destPrefix, char[] nextLayerProtocol, char[] policy);
description: With this function it is possible to manually add a ipsec policy to the security policy database of the current network stack. The network stack will check the selectors and if it finds a match the traffic will be handled with the given policy.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The address and port of the source selector.

parameters.name: sourceFix
parameters.description: The prefix of the source selector. This defines which part of the address of the selector is relevant for filtering.

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: The address and port of the destination selector.

parameters.name: destPrefix
parameters.description: The prefix of the destination sector. This defines which part of the address of the selector is relevant for filtering.

parameters.name: nextLayerProtocol
parameters.description: The upper layer protocol to be used. Can be one of the following protocols: IP_PROTO_ICMP IP_PROTO_TCP IP_PROTO_UDP IP_PROTO_ICMPv6 ANY

parameters.name: <direction> discard
parameters.description: This policy will discard all packets which fits the given selectors.

parameters.name: <direction> none
parameters.description: This policy will do nothing with the packets that fits the given selectors.

parameters.name: <direction>: in|out
parameters.description: in: policy for incoming data out: policy for outgoing data

parameters.name: <protocol>: ah|esp
parameters.description: ah: use authentication header esp: use encapsulating security payload

parameters.name: <mode>: tunnel|transport
parameters.description: tunnel: use tunnel mode. This requires a next parameter of src-dst. transport: use transport mode.

parameters.name: [src-dst]
parameters.description: Is only given if the mode is tunnel src: source address of the tunnel dst: destination address of the tunnel

parameters.name: <level>: use|require
parameters.description: use: use the given policy if a security association is available. Otherwise bypass IPsec. require: always use IPsec. Discard the packet if no security association for the current connection is available.

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
